selected_plan.rationale: Prefer the smallest change that could satisfy the objective with minimal blast radius.

verification_plan.description: Run the narrowest command or test that directly checks the requested behavior.

candidate_plans.rationale: Prefer the smallest change that could satisfy the objective with minimal blast radius.

candidate_plans.rationale: Spend additional effort to understand the underlying failure mode before editing.

verification_plan.description: Read the relevant implementation and documentation before mutating.
Verify behavior with a runtime command or focused test after the change.

candidate_plans.rationale: If confidence stays too low, stop mutation and return evidence plus options.

verification_plan.description: Collect evidence without mutating if the state remains too uncertain.

candidate_plans.rationale: Stabilize expected behavior with explicit checks before trusting a repair.

verification_plan.description: Run the documented examples or existing targeted checks first.
Re-run those checks after the change and compare outputs.

proof_summary.objective: Read req.md carefully. It contains the full requirements for a flight booking website. Build a working implementation of this website. Use Next.js 14 (App Router) with TypeScript for the frontend, and a Node.js/Express backend with PostgreSQL. Create the full project structure, database schema, API routes, and frontend pages. Focus on the core booking flow: flight search, results listing, booking with passenger details, payment mock, confirmation, and booking management. Include an admin panel skeleton. Make it actually runnable with npm install && npm run dev.
